message:
refactor: migrate lexical index to frankensearch facade and retire Cost analytics view

== src/search/tantivy.rs — major delegation to frankensearch::lexical ==

Replace the entire local Tantivy index implementation with thin wrappers
around the frankensearch::lexical crate's CassTantivyIndex.  This removes
~1,150 lines of duplicated logic:

- TantivyIndex now holds an inner FsCassTantivyIndex and delegates
  open_or_create, delete_all, commit, reader, segment_count,
  merge_status, optimize_if_idle, and force_merge through it.
- Schema construction (build_schema), field extraction (fields_from_schema),
  tokenizer registration (ensure_tokenizer), and index directory resolution
  (index_dir) all forward to the fs_* counterparts from frankensearch.
- The local MergeStatus struct, merge constants (MERGE_COOLDOWN_MS,
  MERGE_SEGMENT_THRESHOLD), global LAST_MERGE_TS atomic, SCHEMA_VERSION
  constant, and the FNV hash helper are all removed — now owned by the
  frankensearch crate.
- Document construction moves from inline tantivy doc! macros to building
  Vec<FsCassDocument> structs and calling inner.add_cass_documents().
  Provenance fields (source_id, origin_kind, origin_host) and workspace
  metadata are mapped into the FsCassDocument fields.
- Edge-ngram generation (generate_edge_ngrams), preview building
  (build_preview), and their associated constants (MAX_NGRAM_INDICES) are
  deleted locally; tests now call the frankensearch public APIs directly.
- The large test suite (corruption handling, rebuild scenarios, roundtrip,
  field validation, large batch, etc.) is replaced with minimal smoke tests
  that exercise the facade: open_or_create_roundtrip, schema_hash_matches,
  generate_edge_ngrams_prefixes, build_preview_truncates_with_ellipsis,
  merge_status_api_is_exposed, should_merge logic, index_dir versioned
  path, and tokenizer_registration_is_callable.
- Error mapping uses a local map_fs_err helper converting
  frankensearch::SearchError into anyhow::Error.

== src/search/query.rs — simplify semantic filter adapter ==

Remove the pre-computed allowed_doc_id_hashes optimization from
FsSemanticFilterAdapter:

- The adapter no longer stores an Option<HashSet<u64>> of allowed doc ID
  hashes; instead it relies solely on field-by-field matching via
  parse_fs_semantic_doc_id at query time.
- matches_doc_id_hash() now always returns None (no fast-path hash check),
  and the _doc_id_hash parameter is prefixed with underscore.
- The fs_doc_id_hash() FNV-1a helper function is deleted entirely.
- build_fs_semantic_filter_adapter() no longer takes an &VectorIndex
  parameter and no longer iterates index rows to build the hash set;
  it only checks semantic_filter_is_unrestricted and returns the adapter.
- SemanticSearchState drops its `index: VectorIndex` field since it was
  only used for this pre-filtering.
- Two call sites in SearchClient (ANN path and brute-force path) updated
  to pass only &semantic_filter instead of (&state.index, &semantic_filter).
- Test constructors for FsSemanticFilterAdapter remove the
  allowed_doc_id_hashes field; build_fs_semantic_filter_adapter test
  updated to pass only &filter.

== src/ui/analytics_charts.rs — remove Cost KPI tile ==

In render_kpi_tiles(), remove the conditional branch that displayed a
"Cost (USD)" tile when total_cost_usd > 0.001.  The third tile in
row 2 now always renders the "API Cvg" (coverage percentage) tile
unconditionally with its light-blue/muted-blue color scheme and no
sparkline data.

== src/ui/app.rs — deprecate Cost view in favor of Coverage ==

- Add AnalyticsView::canonical() method that maps Cost -> Coverage,
  leaving all other views unchanged.
- Apply canonical() in the CassMsg::AnalyticsViewChanged handler so
  any attempt to switch to Cost is silently redirected to Coverage.
- Update the AnalyticsTarget::Cost arm in the deep-link/navigation
  mapping to resolve to AnalyticsView::Coverage instead of Cost.
- Change the telemetry/analytics view name for Cost from "analytics:cost"
  to "analytics:coverage" so logs are consistent.
- Update the tab color for the (now-vestigial) Cost variant to match
  Coverage's green (rgb(190, 220, 130)) instead of the old yellow.
- Separate the keyboard shortcut rendering for Cost into its own match
  arm (preserving the same shortcuts) to keep the pattern exhaustive.
- Update two tests: AnalyticsViewChanged(Cost) now asserts the view
  resolves to Coverage; the telemetry name mapping test expects
  "analytics:coverage" for the Cost variant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -534,7 +534,7 @@ fn render_kpi_tiles(data: &AnalyticsChartData, area: Rect, frame: &mut ftui::Fra
         frame,
     );
 
-    // Row 2: Content Tokens | Plan Messages | Cost / Coverage
+    // Row 2: Content Tokens | Plan Messages | Coverage
     let cols2 = Flex::horizontal()
         .constraints([
             Constraint::Percentage(33.0),
@@ -562,28 +562,15 @@ fn render_kpi_tiles(data: &AnalyticsChartData, area: Rect, frame: &mut ftui::Fra
         frame,
     );
 
-    // Cost tile or coverage fallback
-    if data.total_cost_usd > 0.001 {
-        render_kpi_tile(
-            "Cost (USD)",
-            &format!("${:.2}", data.total_cost_usd),
-            &data.daily_cost,
-            PackedRgba::rgb(255, 80, 80), // red
-            PackedRgba::rgb(180, 40, 40), // dark red
-            cols2[2],
-            frame,
-        );
-    } else {
-        render_kpi_tile(
-            "API Cvg",
-            &format!("{:.0}%", data.coverage_pct),
-            &[],                            // no sparkline for coverage
-            PackedRgba::rgb(150, 200, 255), // light blue
-            PackedRgba::rgb(80, 120, 180),  // muted blue
-            cols2[2],
-            frame,
-        );
-    }
+    render_kpi_tile(
+        "API Cvg",
+        &format!("{:.0}%", data.coverage_pct),
+        &[],                            // no sparkline for coverage
+        PackedRgba::rgb(150, 200, 255), // light blue
+        PackedRgba::rgb(80, 120, 180),  // muted blue
+        cols2[2],
+        frame,
+    );
 }
 
 /// Render a single KPI tile: label (dim) + value (bright) + mini sparkline.
